tskb.explorer.core.desc: CLI-side explorer layer: graph→chunk transform, HTTP server, and static export

files.name: export.ts
files.desc: Copies pre-built SPA assets to an output directory and writes all chunk JSON files alongside them, producing a fully self-contained static explorer that works offline via file://

files.name: server.ts
files.desc: Node built-in HTTP server that serves the pre-built SPA assets and generates chunk JSON responses on demand. Reads graph.json once, transforms it, and caches all chunk JSON strings in memory for the lifetime of the process.

files.name: transform.ts
files.desc: Converts KnowledgeGraph into ExplorerChunks: a MetaChunk (root, topFolders, docs, flows, terms, externals) plus one FolderChunk per folder that has modules or sub-folders. Two ghost injection passes: buildGhostIntermediaryChains() synthesizes FolderChunks for every filesystem directory that lies between a declared folder's path and its owned modules/subfolders; injectGhostNodes() adds ghost ExplorerNodes for any .ts/.tsx files recorded in folder.children.files that are not declared as modules.

tskb.explorer.app.desc: Vite SPA source for the interactive explorer UI. Built separately from the library (npm run build:explorer) and shipped in dist/explorer/

tskb.explorer.app.components.desc: Node and edge rendering components for the D3 canvas. Each node type implements NodeComponent; edges are drawn by EdgeRenderer

files.name: BoundaryRenderer.ts
files.desc: renderBoundaryGroups() draws a freeform dashed hull outline around all visible nodes that belong to the same architectural boundary. Hierarchy resolution: only the top-level folder carries detail.boundary; all descendants are resolved by walking parentOf. Hull is computed via d3.polygonHull, perturbed with seeded pseudo-random wobble for a stable hand-drawn look, and smoothed with curveBasisClosed.

tskb.explorer.app.graph.desc: SPA data layer: chunk type registry, type-safe loader with LRU cache

files.name: chunk-types.ts
files.desc: Discriminated union types for all chunk shapes (MetaChunk, FolderChunk) plus the ChunkRegistry map and a typed URL builder. Extend ChunkRegistry here to add new chunk kinds.

files.name: loader.ts
files.desc: ChunkLoader: type-safe generic fetch keyed by ChunkKind, LRU-bounded in-memory cache (50 chunks), deduplication of in-flight requests

tskb.explorer.app.layout.desc: Lane layout engine: computes SVG positions for structure (d3.tree), docs, and other lanes

files.name: lane-engine.ts
files.desc: computeLayout(): builds a d3.hierarchy from visible structure nodes, runs d3.tree for left-to-right positioning, then lays docs and other nodes as horizontal lists below. Collapsed folders with _childCount > 0 inject up to 4 ghost placeholder nodes so the tree reserves space for their children.

tskb.explorer.app.store.desc: Pure graph data store (loaded chunks only). UI state lives outside the store in main.ts

files.name: graph-store.ts
files.desc: GraphStore: pure data store holding meta chunk and folder chunks. UI state (expanded set, selected node, search query) lives outside in main.ts

tskb.explorer.app.ui.desc: UI shell components: global spinner, per-node spinner, node hover tooltip, and code preview popup

files.name: CodeTooltip.ts
files.desc: Toggle-based code preview popup for module nodes. Click the <> bubble to show/hide; clicking the same node again dismisses it; clicking outside also dismisses. Renders morphology lines with an inline TypeScript tokenizer (no runtime deps) for syntax highlighting, prepended by import lines with a blank-line separator.

files.name: NodeTooltip.ts
files.desc: Hover tooltip anchored to the node's right-center SVG position. Shows a coloured dot, node name, monospace path, and description. Receives updateNodeTooltipTransform() calls from main.ts on every zoom event so it repositions to track the node. Uses mouseover/mouseout bubbling with relatedTarget boundary guards to avoid flicker on child SVG elements.

files.name: Spinner.ts
files.desc: Global and per-node spinner helpers: showGlobalSpinner/hideGlobalSpinner overlay a full-screen loader; showNodeSpinner/removeNodeSpinner attach a small SVG spinner next to a node while its chunk is loading

core.visualization.desc: Graphviz DOT visualization generator - transforms the knowledge graph into a hierarchical diagram

files.name: dot-generator.ts
files.desc: DOT file generator - renders folders as nested subgraphs, modules as ellipses, terms as diamonds, docs as notes, with colored edges for relationship types

library.desc: Refers to the <TSKB> package with all of its capabilities, interfaces and commands

cli.desc: The library exposes a command line interface for main actions - build docs to json graph, generate visualization, search nodes from the graph by query

jsxRuntime.desc: TSKB provides a custom JSX runtime (jsx-runtime module) that transforms TSX documentation syntax into a knowledge graph. Configured via tsconfig's jsxImportSource option, it processes JSX elements differently from React - instead of DOM rendering, it extracts documentation structure, references, and code snippets for analysis and visualization.

tsProgram.desc: TypeScript compiler Program from source files and tsconfig settings that enables static analysis of code structure, types, and symbols without emitting JavaScript output.

registry.desc: The global namespace (declare global { namespace tskb }) containing typed interfaces (Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms) that serve as a type-safe vocabulary registry, enabling autocomplete and type-checked references across all .tskb.tsx documentation files.

graph.desc: Refers to th raw output of the library, where it constructs all the relations and connections between folders, modules, exports, terms, docs, etc. In its raw form it is a json file

dotFile.desc: A Graphviz DOT format file that describes the graph structure with nodes and edges, used for rendering visual diagrams of the architecture

searchResult.desc: Result from searching the knowledge graph - includes ranked matches with scores across the entire graph, found via fuzzy matching on IDs, descriptions, and paths

knowledgeChunk.desc: An on-demand JSON fragment of the knowledge graph. meta.json contains root folder, top-level folder summaries, docs, flows, and terms. folder-{id}.json contains modules, exports, sub-folders, and import edges for one folder.

explorerLane.desc: A horizontal section of the explorer SVG canvas. Three lanes stack vertically: Structure (code hierarchy, d3.tree), Each lane has a labeled background band.

nodeComponent.desc: Interface for rendering a node type in the D3 canvas: enter() appends SVG elements, update() repositions them, anchor() returns edge connection points, getSize() returns bounding box. Implemented by BaseNodeRenderer; override per type.

lruChunkCache.desc: Bounded LRU cache inside ChunkLoader (max 50 entries). Map insertion order is used for O(1) LRU: on get, entry is deleted and re-inserted; on set overflow, oldest key is evicted. Chunks are immutable so no invalidation is needed.

explorerStaticExport.desc: Self-contained static export produced by `tskb explore --export`. Contains the pre-built SPA (index.html + hashed JS chunks) and all graph chunk JSON files. Works offline via file:// because the SPA uses relative fetch paths.

ghostNode.desc: A placeholder ExplorerNode with detail._ghost = 'true'. Ghost nodes represent filesystem directories or files visible in the tree that are not declared in the knowledge graph. Two sources: (1) buildGhostIntermediaryChains() in transform.ts synthesizes ghost FolderChunks for every intermediate filesystem directory between a declared folder's path and its owned modules; (2) injectGhostNodes() in transform.ts adds ghost module/folder nodes from folder.children scanner data. Lane-engine also injects transient ghost placeholders for collapsed folders with _childCount > 0 to pre-reserve tree space. All ghost nodes render as dashed transparent cards with only a filename label.

ast.desc: Abstract Syntax Tree - TypeScript's tree representation of source code, used by the compiler API to analyze code structure

declarationMerging.desc: TypeScript feature that allows multiple declarations with the same name to be merged into a single definition, enabling distributed vocabulary across files

typeChecker.desc: TypeScript compiler component that resolves types, validates code, and provides type information for symbols in the AST

literalType.desc: A TypeScript type that represents a specific literal value (e.g., string literal 'hello' or number literal 42), used to extract constant values from type definitions

jsxElement.desc:
A JSX syntax node in the TypeScript AST representing an element like or

, containing opening tag, children, and closing tag

jsxExpression.desc: A TypeScript AST node representing an embedded expression in JSX curly braces like {ref as tskb.Folders['Auth']}

typeAssertion.desc: TypeScript 'as' syntax that casts a value to a specific type, used in tskb to reference vocabulary items: {ref as tskb.Modules['X']}

depthFirstTraversal.desc: Tree traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking, used to visit all JSX nodes in order

cliPipeline.desc: The build process: file discovery → program initialization → registry extraction → doc extraction → graph construction → output generation (JSON, DOT, skill/instructions)

commandRouting.desc: The CLI's mechanism for parsing arguments and delegating to the appropriate command handler (init, build, search, pick, context, ls, docs, or flows)

globPattern.desc: File pattern syntax (e.g., '**/*.tskb.tsx') used to match documentation files for processing

folderIdNavigation.desc: Navigation strategy using folder IDs from the knowledge graph registry (e.g., 'tskb.cli', 'Package.Root') instead of filesystem paths

tskbOutputDir.desc: The .tskb/ directory containing all build outputs: graph.json and graph.dot

tskbInit.desc: Interactive scaffolder command that creates docs folder, tsconfig, starter doc, package.json script, and opt-in AI integration directories

resolvedVia.desc: Resolution strategy used by resolveNode: 'id' (exact match), 'path' (filesystem path match), or 'nearest-parent' (deepest folder whose path is a prefix of the identifier)

repositoryPattern.desc: Data access abstraction layer that separates business logic from database operations

serviceLayer.desc: Business logic layer that orchestrates operations between controllers and repositories

contextProvider.desc: React pattern for managing and sharing state across component tree without prop drilling

jwt.desc: JSON Web Token - stateless authentication mechanism using signed tokens

pagination.desc: Technique for splitting large datasets into smaller pages for efficient data transfer

middlewareChain.desc: Sequential processing pattern where request passes through multiple handlers

dependencyInjection.desc: Design pattern where dependencies are provided to a class rather than created internally

crudOperations.desc: Create, Read, Update, Delete - fundamental data operations

typeGuards.desc: TypeScript runtime checks that narrow types within conditional blocks

Folder.desc: A generic helper to reference folders inside doc files

Module.desc: A generic helper to reference modules inside doc files

Export.desc: A generic helper to reference exports inside doc files

Term.desc: A generic helper to reference terms inside doc files

File.desc: A generic helper to reference non-JS/TS files (README.md, yml configs, etc.) inside doc files

ref.desc: A placeholder for referencing anything from the global tskb registry into jsx documentation tags

docs/src/tskb/tests.tskb.tsx.explains: E2E test infrastructure: Vitest suite, fixture project, and CLI validation strategy
docs/src/tskb/tests.tskb.tsx.content:
E2E Test Infrastructure The test suite ( <nodeId: tests>) validates the entire tskb CLI by running it as a subprocess
      against a realistic fixture project. Tests use Vitest and spawn the built CLI binary via
      Node's execFileSync, asserting on stdout and generated artifacts. <flow name="e2e-test-execution" desc="Full E2E run: global setup builds fixture graph, test files exercise every CLI command, teardown cleans output"><step order="0" nodeId="vitest.config" label="Discovers test files, registers global setup" /><step order="1" nodeId="tests.global-setup" label="Builds fixture graph once via tskb build" /><step order="2" nodeId="tests.helpers" label="Provides CLI runners and graph loader to all test files" /><step order="3" nodeId="tests.global-setup" label="Teardown cleans .tskb/ output directory" /></flow><flow name="init-scaffolding-test" desc="How the init command is tested: copies fixture to temp dir, runs init, asserts generated files"><step order="0" nodeId="tests.helpers" label="Copies fixture src/ into a temp directory" /><step order="1" nodeId="cli.build" label="Runs tskb init --yes in the temp directory" /><step order="2" nodeId="tests.e2e" label="Asserts scaffolded files: tsconfig, starter doc, scripts, AI dirs" /></flow>Fixture Project The fixture at <nodeId: tests.e2e.fixture>is a small task-management TypeScript app with models,
      services, API routes, and utilities. It includes its own tskb docs folder with 6 doc files
      covering all three priority levels (essential, constraint, supplementary) and all registry
      primitives (Folder, Module, Export, Term, External, Relation, Flow). This makes it a
      comprehensive test subject for the full build and query pipeline. Test Structure Tests are split into focused files under <nodeId: tests.e2e>, sharing utilities from <nodeId: tests.helpers>. A
      Vitest global setup ( <nodeId: tests.global-setup>) builds the fixture graph once before any test runs. <nodeId: tests.init>— scaffolding tests with idempotency checks <nodeId: tests.build>— graph output validation: node counts, priorities, edges <nodeId: tests.commands>— exercises every query command in plain and JSON modes <nodeId: tests.disambiguation>— ambiguous ID resolution across commands <nodeId: tests.graph-integrity>— edge consistency, type signatures, external metadata Running Tests Tests require the package to be built first (npm run build). Run with npm test (vitest run) or
      npm run test:watch (vitest) for interactive mode. The 60-second timeout in <nodeId: vitest.config>accounts for CLI subprocess startup cost.

docs/src/tskb/main.tskb.tsx.explains: Architecture, API surface, and usage flow of the TSKB library
docs/src/tskb/main.tskb.tsx.content:
Architecture and implementation docs for the <TSKB> library The package is located in <nodeId: TSKB.Package.Root>, with its <nodeId: package.json>and <nodeId: npm.README.md>for npm. What is <TSKB> ? A TypeScript DSL for type-safe architectural documentation. Generates queryable knowledge
      graphs and diagrams with type-checked references. What it provides in terms of interfaces (API's) and tools In the root folder: <nodeId: TSKB.Package.Root>. In the <nodeId: package.json>file, it declares a bin command named 'tskb' that runs the <nodeId: cli>The package exports two main modules, in addition to the <nodeId: cli>: the index module   <nodeId: Main.index.js>and the jsxRuntime ( <nodeId: jsxRuntime>), defined in: <nodeId: Jsx.runtime.js>Core Primitives for Type-Safe Documentation Main exports (primitives) of the library for creating type-safe architectural documentation: <nodeId: Folder>: Represents logical groupings in the codebase (features, layers, packages) <nodeId: Module>: Represents concrete code units (classes, services, components) <nodeId: Export>: Creates type-safe references to actual code exports using TypeScript's
          typeof import() syntax - ensures documentation stays in sync with codebase <nodeId: Term>: Represents domain concepts, patterns, or terminology <nodeId: File>: References non-JS/TS files (README.md, yml configs, etc.) by path <nodeId: ref>: A placeholder for referencing anything from the global tskb registry into jsx
          tags with type assertions Plus all the JSX documentation components exported from <nodeId: Jsx.runtime.js>: Doc, H1, H2, H3,
      P, List, Li, Snippet - providing a familiar TSX syntax for structured content A typical flow of usage Create a docs folder in your repository for documentation files Define a tsconfig.json similar to <nodeId: sample.tsconfig.json>pointing to repo root with jsx:
        "react-jsx" and jsxImportSource: "tskb" Declare vocabulary (Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms, Files) using global namespace
        augmentation. Use typeof import() for type-safe references. Write *.tskb.tsx files using JSX components. TypeScript validates all references at compile
        time. Run <nodeId: cli.build>: "tskb \"**/*.tskb.tsx\" --tsconfig tsconfig.json" The <nodeId: cli>creates a <nodeId: tsProgram>and type-checks all documentation files against
        actual source code <relation from="tsProgram" to="typescript.program" /><relation from="tsProgram" to="ref" />Extracts vocabulary from the <nodeId: registry>(Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms) Processes *.tskb.tsx files, extracting content and references Constructs the <nodeId: graph>by analyzing relationships and hierarchies Outputs JSON knowledge graph optimized for AI agents and tooling Generating a visual representation: <nodeId: cli.build>automatically generates a <nodeId: dotFile>in .tskb/graph.dot using   <nodeId: generateDot><nodeId: generateDot>transforms the <nodeId: graph>into a <nodeId: dotFile>with nodes and
        relationship edges Render with Graphviz: "dot -Tpng .tskb/graph.dot -o .tskb/graph.png" Or use interactive viewers like xdot or online Graphviz tools Key Benefits Type-checked references: Renames, moves, or API changes break the build - enforcing
        correctness Validated snippets: Code examples are type-checked against actual source Living documentation: Stays in sync through the compiler - CI catches drift AI-optimized: Queryable knowledge graph reduces hallucination Docs as infrastructure: Compile-time validation, not afterthought Navigating the knowledge graph Three commands for exploring and querying the knowledge graph - optimized for AI agents List all folders Run <nodeId: cli.ls>: "tskb ls ./dist/taskflow-graph.json" or "tskb ls
        ./dist/taskflow-graph.json --depth 2" Always starts from Package.Root, lists all folders hierarchically Controllable depth: default 1 (immediate children), -1 for unlimited Returns flat JSON with folder ID, depth level, description, and path Use for initial orientation and discovering folder IDs Pick any node Run <nodeId: cli.pick>: "tskb pick tskb.cli" (by ID) or "tskb pick packages/tskb/src/cli" (by
        path) Resolves any node type: folder, module, export, term, or doc Returns type-specific data: parent, children, modules, exports, referencing docs Falls back to nearest parent folder when no exact match is found Search the graph Run <nodeId: cli.search>: "tskb search auth" or "tskb search build command" Fuzzy searches the entire graph across IDs, descriptions, and paths using Fuse.js <nodeId: searchResult>includes: top 10 ranked results with score (0-1, higher = better match) Searches across all node types: folders, modules, exports, terms, and docs Use pick to get full details on any result Documentation Philosophy: Map, Not Manual Write structural signposts showing what exists where and how pieces connect - not
      implementation details. Document structure: Declare folders, locations, and key relationships Use type-safe references: typeof import() ensures TypeScript validates existence and catches
        renames Focus on WHAT and WHERE, not HOW: Describe purpose and location, not algorithms Trust validation: TSKB catches broken references, not behavior changes Major relationships only: Document architectural connections, not every dependency <flow name="build-pipeline" desc="The tskb build process: source files through extraction to knowledge graph outputs"><step order="0" nodeId="cli.build" label="Orchestrates the pipeline: file discovery, extraction, graph building, output generation" /><step order="1" nodeId="extractRegistry" label="Scans TypeScript AST for global tskb namespace declarations (Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms)" /><step order="2" nodeId="extractDocs" label="Traverses JSX trees in .tskb.tsx files, extracting content, references, relations, and flows" /><step order="3" nodeId="buildGraph" label="Merges registry and docs into a unified graph with nodes, edges, and hierarchy" /><step order="4" nodeId="generateDot" label="Transforms the graph into DOT format for Graphviz visualization" /></flow>

docs/src/tskb/typescript/typescript.tskb.explains: TypeScript Program creation for static analysis without compilation
docs/src/tskb/typescript/typescript.tskb.content: TypeScript Program Located in <nodeId: tskb.typescript>. <nodeId: typescript.program>exports <nodeId: ts.createProgram>which creates a   <nodeId: tsProgram>for analyzing code without compilation (noEmit=true). Reads and parses tsconfig.json for compiler options Validates TypeScript errors in specified files Returns Program with AST, type checker, and symbol resolution <flow name="static-analysis" desc="TypeScript Program creation through extraction to graph"><step order="0" nodeId="ts.createProgram" label="creates Program from tsconfig + source files" /><step order="1" nodeId="extraction.registry" label="walks AST for Folders, Modules, Terms, Exports" /><step order="2" nodeId="extraction.documentation" label="parses JSX trees for content and references" /><step order="3" nodeId="graph.builder" label="assembles nodes and edges into knowledge graph" /></flow>

docs/src/tskb/runtime/runtime.tskb.tsx.explains: Runtime module structure: JSX primitives and registry type definitions
docs/src/tskb/runtime/runtime.tskb.tsx.content:
Runtime Located in <nodeId: tskb.runtime>. Contains registry type definitions and JSX primitives - no actual
      runtime execution. Modules <nodeId: runtime.jsx>: JSX runtime with <nodeId: jsx.Doc>(requires explains prop and optional priority via   <nodeId: DocPriority>: essential, constraint, or supplementary). Includes
        heading/paragraph/list components, <nodeId: jsx.Adr>for Architecture Decision Records, and   <nodeId: jsx.ref>for type-safe registry references. <nodeId: runtime.jsx>also provides <nodeId: jsx.Flow>and <nodeId: jsx.Step>for defining named, ordered
        sequences through the system. Flows become first-class graph nodes with priority support.
        Only Step children are allowed inside Flow (validated at build time). <nodeId: runtime.registry>: Type definitions for Folder, Module, Export, Term interfaces used in
        global namespace augmentation. Purpose Provides TypeScript types and JSX functions for authoring *.tskb.tsx files. Registry
      interfaces enable type-safe vocabulary declarations. JSX components structure documentation
      content. Semantic Relations The Relation tag creates a semantic edge in the knowledge graph between two nodes. It
      is type-safe and supports any registered Folder, Module, Term, or Export. The optional   label prop allows you to describe the relationship (e.g., "depends on"). <relation from="runtime.jsx" to="graph" label="runtime describes graph" />This will emit a related-to edge from the runtime.jsx module to the Graph   term in the TSKB graph, with the label "runtime describes graph".

docs/src/tskb/explorer/transform.tskb.ts.explains: Transform: how KnowledgeGraph becomes ExplorerChunks — edge indexing, meta assembly, recursive folder chunking, ghost injection
docs/src/tskb/explorer/transform.tskb.ts.content:
Graph Transform <nodeId: explorer.transform>is the single step between the raw knowledge graph and what the browser
      receives. <nodeId: explorer.transformGraph>is implemented as a class ( GraphToExplorerTransformer ) whose transform() method orchestrates
      all steps — edge indexing, meta assembly, folder chunk recursion, ghost injection, and child
      count patching. Output shape The output is ExplorerChunks : a MetaChunk plus a   Map<folderId, FolderChunk> . The server and exporter serialize each entry to
      a separate <nodeId: knowledgeChunk>JSON file: meta.json and   folder-{sanitizedId}.json . MetaChunk contains: root folder node, top-level folder summaries (with   _hasChildren / _childCount hints), all doc nodes, flow nodes, term
      nodes, external nodes, and cross-lane references edges. FolderChunk contains: direct sub-folders, direct modules, all exports of
      those modules, import edges between modules in this folder ( internalEdges ), and
      import edges that cross the folder boundary ( externalEdges ). Edge lookup indexes Before anything else, the transformer builds two Map<nodeId, GraphEdge[]>   indexes — one keyed by the source node id ( edgesBySource ) and one by the target
      node id ( edgesByTarget ). All subsequent queries run in O(1) per node instead of
      scanning the full edge list. Recursive folder chunking buildAllFolderChunksRecursively() does a depth-first walk starting from the root
      folder, following contains edges. For each folder it finds direct modules (via   belongs-to edges pointing at the folder) and direct sub-folders (via   contains edges from the folder). Folders with neither are skipped but still
      recursed into, so deeply nested folders with content are always reached. Ghost node injection Ghost injection runs in two passes. First, buildGhostIntermediaryChains() detects
      path gaps between a declared folder's path and its owned modules or sub-folders. When a module
      lives at pkg/src/core/server.ts but its declared parent folder has path   pkg , the transformer synthesizes <nodeId: ghostNode>FolderChunk entries
      for every intermediate directory ( pkg/src , pkg/src/core ) and
      re-parents the module to the deepest one. The chain is not synthesized if any intermediate
      path is already owned by a declared folder. Second, injectGhostNodes() scans every declared folder's   folder.children.files and folder.children.folders (scanner data
      recorded during tskb build ). Any .ts / .tsx file not
      already in a chunk becomes a ghost module node. Any direct child directory not already
      represented (either as a declared folder or a ghost intermediary) and not a transparent
      intermediary becomes a ghost folder node. Ghost intermediary chunks are not processed by this
      pass — they only contain what the graph already knows. Child count patching After ghost injection is complete, patchFolderChildCounts() writes the final   _hasChildren and _childCount detail fields on every folder   ExplorerNode . These fields drive the expand button visibility and badge in the
      SPA. The patch runs last so ghost nodes are included in the counts. <flow name="graph-to-chunks-transform" desc="How KnowledgeGraph becomes ExplorerChunks: edge indexing, meta assembly, recursive folder chunking, ghost node injection, child count patching"><step order="0" nodeId="cli.commands.explore" label="Reads graph.json from disk into KnowledgeGraph" /><step order="1" nodeId="explorer.transform" label="groupEdgesByKey() builds edgesBySource + edgesByTarget indexes from all graph edges" /><step order="2" nodeId="explorer.transformGraph" label="Assembles MetaChunk: root node, topFolders, docs, flows, terms, externals, cross-lane reference edges" /><step order="3" nodeId="explorer.transformGraph" label="buildAllFolderChunksRecursively() depth-first: modules, exports, subfolders, internal + external import edges per folder" /><step order="4" nodeId="explorer.transform" label="buildGhostIntermediaryChains() synthesizes FolderChunks for every intermediate filesystem directory between a declared folder path and its owned modules/subfolders" /><step order="5" nodeId="explorer.transform" label="injectGhostNodes() adds placeholder ExplorerNodes for undeclared .ts/.tsx files and undeclared child directories recorded in folder.children scanner data" /><step order="6" nodeId="explorer.transform" label="patchFolderChildCounts() finalizes _hasChildren + _childCount on all folder nodes after ghost injection" /></flow>

docs/src/tskb/explorer/spa.tskb.tsx.explains: Explorer SPA architecture: D3 canvas, chunk loading, three-lane layout, node components, UI shell
docs/src/tskb/explorer/spa.tskb.tsx.content:
Explorer SPA The SPA is built with Vite (D3 in a separate vendor chunk) and lives in the   explorer-app/ directory. It is pre-built into dist/explorer/ and
      shipped inside the tskb npm package. <nodeId: explorer.spa.main>is the entry point that bootstraps the
      canvas, wires all interaction handlers, and drives the render loop. Data layer <nodeId: explorer.spa.chunk-types>defines the chunk discriminated union ( MetaChunk | FolderChunk ) and the ChunkRegistry map that keys chunk kinds to their types. Adding a new
      chunk kind means extending this file only. <nodeId: explorer.spa.loader>provides <nodeId: explorer.spa.ChunkLoader>: a generic load(kind, ...args) whose
      return type is inferred from ChunkRegistry[K] . It deduplicates in-flight requests
      and uses a <nodeId: lruChunkCache>(max 50 entries) so folders are not re-fetched on collapse/expand
      cycles. <nodeId: explorer.spa.GraphStore>in <nodeId: explorer.spa.store>holds only graph data (meta chunk, folder chunks map).
      Data arrives via loadMeta() and loadFolderChunk() . UI state — the
      expanded node set, selected node, and search query — lives as plain variables in <nodeId: explorer.spa.main>  and is passed explicitly to <nodeId: explorer.spa.computeLayout>, <nodeId: explorer.spa.renderStructureEdges>, and   <nodeId: explorer.spa.BaseNodeRenderer>. The store exposes subscribe(listener) for reactive
      re-render. <relation from="explorer.spa.store" to="explorer.spa.chunk-types" label="stores chunks typed by" /><relation from="explorer.spa.loader" to="explorer.spa.chunk-types" label="types fetch results via" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.store" label="subscribes to" />Three-lane layout <nodeId: explorer.spa.lane-engine>computes all SVG positions in a single pass. The canvas has three   <nodeId: explorerLane>s stacked vertically: Structure — builds a d3.hierarchy from the visible tree (root
        → expanded folders → modules → exports, including sub-folder nesting). Runs   d3.tree().nodeSize() for left-to-right positioning. Collapsed folders with   _childCount > 0 inject ghost placeholder nodes so the tree pre-reserves
        space for their children. Docs — horizontal list ordered by priority (essential → constraint →
        supplementary). Terms / Flows — horizontal list below docs. On every expand or collapse, <nodeId: explorer.spa.main>calls <nodeId: explorer.spa.computeLayout>and fully re-renders the
      D3 selection with enter/update/exit. Node components <nodeId: explorer.spa.node-base>defines the <nodeId: nodeComponent>interface and <nodeId: explorer.spa.BaseNodeRenderer>— the
      default renderer for all node types. Each node is an SVG <g> containing a
      rounded-rect card, type icon, label, description, edge-count badge, a <>   code preview bubble (top-center), and a + expand bubble (right edge).   <nodeId: ghostNode>s render as dashed transparent placeholders showing only their filename. <nodeId: explorer.spa.edge-renderer>derives parent→child links via <nodeId: explorer.spa.buildStructureLinks>and draws them
      as horizontal cubic-bezier SVG paths via <nodeId: explorer.spa.renderStructureEdges>. Ghost links (where
      either endpoint has detail._ghost = 'true' ) render with a dashed stroke and
      reduced opacity. It also renders the labeled background bands for each lane. <nodeId: explorer.spa.boundary-renderer>draws architectural boundary outlines. When a folder node carries   detail.boundary , all visible descendants (resolved by walking   parentOf ) are grouped under that boundary name. <nodeId: explorer.spa.renderBoundaryGroups>  computes a convex hull over the bounding boxes of all member nodes, perturbs the hull points
      with a seeded pseudo-random wobble (stable across re-renders), and smooths the result with   d3.curveBasisClosed for a hand-drawn look. The outline renders as a violet dashed
      stroke with a near-transparent fill and a pill label at the top. All elements have   pointer-events: none . <relation from="explorer.spa.node-base" to="explorer.spa.lane-engine" label="reads NODE_SIZES from" /><relation from="explorer.spa.edge-renderer" to="explorer.spa.lane-engine" label="reads LaneLayout from" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.lane-engine" label="calls computeLayout on each render" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.node-base" label="calls enter/update on node selections" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.edge-renderer" label="calls renderStructureEdges" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.boundary-renderer" label="calls renderBoundaryGroups" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.node-tooltip" label="propagates zoom transform to" /><relation from="explorer.spa.main" to="explorer.spa.code-tooltip" label="propagates zoom transform to" /><relation from="explorer.spa.node-base" to="explorer.spa.node-tooltip" label="triggers show/hide on hover" /><relation from="explorer.spa.node-base" to="explorer.spa.code-tooltip" label="triggers toggle on <> click" />UI shell <nodeId: explorer.spa.spinner>provides a full-screen global spinner (shown while meta.json   loads) and a per-node inline spinner: <nodeId: explorer.spa.showNodeSpinner>appends an animated SVG ring at
      the node's canvas position; <nodeId: explorer.spa.removeNodeSpinner>removes it in the finally   block after the chunk fetch settles. <nodeId: explorer.spa.node-tooltip>appears on hover, anchored to the node's right-center SVG position.   <nodeId: explorer.spa.code-tooltip>is a click-toggled code preview popup anchored to the node's top-center
      SVG position. Both receive updateXxxTransform() calls from <nodeId: explorer.spa.main>on every
      D3 zoom event so they reposition to track their anchor nodes as the canvas pans and zooms. <flow name="explorer-expand-flow" desc="User expands a folder node: chunk is fetched, store updated, layout recomputed, SVG re-rendered"><step order="0" nodeId="explorer.spa.node-base" label="User clicks + expand button; BaseNodeRenderer fires the onExpand callback" /><step order="1" nodeId="explorer.spa.main" label="ExplorerApp.onExpand(): calls showNodeSpinner, delegates to loader, calls removeNodeSpinner in finally; adds node id to expanded set" /><step order="2" nodeId="explorer.spa.loader" label="ChunkLoader.load('folder', id) — returns cached chunk or fetches /chunks/folder-{sanitizedId}.json" /><step order="3" nodeId="explorer.spa.store" label="store.loadFolderChunk() stores chunk; store notifies subscribers, triggering ExplorerApp.render()" /><step order="4" nodeId="explorer.spa.computeLayout" label="render() calls computeLayout(store, expanded) — rebuilds d3.hierarchy and recomputes all SVG positions" /><step order="5" nodeId="explorer.spa.buildStructureLinks" label="render() calls buildStructureLinks() — derives parent→child link list from updated parentId fields" /><step order="6" nodeId="explorer.spa.renderStructureEdges" label="render() calls renderStructureEdges() — redraws cubic-bezier SVG paths; ghost links rendered dashed" /><step order="7" nodeId="explorer.spa.node-base" label="render() D3 enter/update/exit — new node cards enter with fade-in, collapsed nodes exit with fade-out" /></flow>

docs/src/tskb/explorer/serve.tskb.tsx.explains: Explorer HTTP server: chunk API, in-memory cache, SPA asset serving, auto-open browser
docs/src/tskb/explorer/serve.tskb.tsx.content:
Explorer Server <nodeId: explorer.serveExplorer>in <nodeId: explorer.server>starts a Node built-in http server — no
      external dependency. It runs the graph transform once at startup, serializes every <nodeId: knowledgeChunk>  to JSON, and keeps all of them in a Map<string, string> for the lifetime of
      the process. Subsequent chunk requests are pure string writes with no re-serialization. Request routing Two routes are handled: /chunks/*.json — chunk API. The key is extracted from the pathname (e.g.   meta or folder-tskb.core ) and looked up in the in-memory cache. A
      404 is returned if the key is unknown. /* (static assets) — SPA files are served from dist/explorer/ ,
      resolved via import.meta.url so the path stays correct after   npm pack . Any unknown path falls back to index.html so the SPA
      router handles client-side navigation. A path traversal guard rejects any request that
      resolves outside dist/explorer/ . Command entry point <nodeId: explorer.explore>in <nodeId: cli.commands.explore>locates graph.json via   <nodeId: cli.utils.graph-finder>, parses it, then branches: if --export is provided it calls   exportExplorer ; otherwise it calls <nodeId: explorer.serveExplorer>. The module is
      dynamically imported so the SPA assets directory is only resolved when actually needed. <flow name="explorer-serve-flow" desc="tskb explore: CLI finds graph, transforms chunks, starts HTTP server, browser loads SPA and fetches chunks on demand"><step order="0" nodeId="explorer.explore" label="Reads graph.json, branches on --export flag" /><step order="1" nodeId="explorer.transformGraph" label="Converts KnowledgeGraph into MetaChunk + FolderChunks, serializes all to JSON strings" /><step order="2" nodeId="explorer.serveExplorer" label="Starts http server: /chunks/*.json reads from in-memory cache; /* serves SPA assets" /><step order="3" nodeId="explorer.spa.main" label="ExplorerApp.mount(): setupCanvas (D3 SVG + zoom/pan), setupRenderer, setupSearch, then loadInitialData fetches meta.json" /><step order="4" nodeId="explorer.spa.lane-engine" label="computeLayout() positions all visible nodes across three lanes" /><step order="5" nodeId="explorer.spa.node-base" label="D3 enter/update renders node cards into SVG groups" /></flow>

docs/src/tskb/explorer/export.tskb.tsx.explains: Explorer static export: chunk file layout, SPA asset copy, file:// compatibility
docs/src/tskb/explorer/export.tskb.tsx.content:
Explorer Static Export <nodeId: explorer.exportExplorer>in <nodeId: explorer.export>produces a <nodeId: explorerStaticExport>: a directory containing
      the pre-built SPA ( index.html + hashed JS/CSS assets) and all <nodeId: knowledgeChunk>JSON
      files written to a chunks/ sub-directory. Output layout The default output directory is .tskb/explorer/ . The structure mirrors what the
      server serves dynamically: index.html , assets/ — Vite-built SPA with content-hashed filenames. chunks/meta.json — MetaChunk: root, top-level folders, docs, flows, terms,
      externals. chunks/folder-{sanitizedId}.json — one FolderChunk per folder with
      modules or sub-folders. File:// compatibility The SPA fetches chunks using relative paths ( ./chunks/meta.json ) rather than
      absolute server routes. This means the exported output works when opened directly in a browser
      via file:// without running a server, making it suitable for archiving or sharing
      as a zip. <flow name="explorer-export-flow" desc="tskb explore --export: CLI finds graph, transforms all chunks, copies SPA assets, writes chunk JSON files to disk"><step order="0" nodeId="explorer.explore" label="Reads graph.json, detects --export flag, resolves output directory (default: .tskb/explorer)" /><step order="1" nodeId="explorer.transformGraph" label="Converts KnowledgeGraph into MetaChunk + FolderChunks" /><step order="2" nodeId="explorer.export" label="copyDir() recursively copies dist/explorer/ SPA assets to output directory" /><step order="3" nodeId="explorer.exportExplorer" label="Writes chunks/meta.json and chunks/folder-{id}.json for every folder chunk" /></flow>

docs/src/tskb/explorer/explorer.tskb.tsx.explains: tskb Explorer: interactive visual graph browser opened via `tskb explore`. Architecture, data flow, SPA layout, and extension points.
docs/src/tskb/explorer/explorer.tskb.tsx.content:
Explorer The explorer is a D3-powered SPA that renders the knowledge graph as a visual, interactive
      diagram. It opens via tskb explore (local HTTP server) or exports to a
      self-contained static folder via tskb explore --export . The implementation splits into two layers: the CLI-side layer in <nodeId: tskb.explorer.core>(graph
      transform, HTTP server, static export) and the Vite SPA in <nodeId: tskb.explorer.app>(D3 canvas,
      chunk loading, layout, node components). CLI layer The CLI layer has three focused modules — see their dedicated docs for details: <nodeId: explorer.transform>( <nodeId: explorer.transformGraph>) — converts a flat KnowledgeGraph into   ExplorerChunks : a MetaChunk plus one FolderChunk per
      folder that has modules or sub-folders. Ghost nodes are injected for filesystem files not
      declared in the graph. <nodeId: explorer.server>( <nodeId: explorer.serveExplorer>) — Node built-in http server that caches
      all chunk JSON strings in memory after the first transform and serves them at   /chunks/*.json . No external server dependency. <nodeId: explorer.export>( <nodeId: explorer.exportExplorer>) — copies the pre-built SPA assets to an output
      directory and writes all chunk JSON files alongside them, producing a fully self-contained
      static explorer. Build integration npm run build:explorer runs Vite, outputting to dist/explorer/ .   npm run build:lib runs tsc , outputting to dist/ . The
      full npm run build runs explorer first, then lib — so the SPA assets are inside   dist/ when the package is published. During development, npm run dev:explorer starts Vite's HMR dev server against a
      fixed chunks/ directory. The CLI build:lib must be run at least once
      so the dist/ server code exists.

docs/src/tskb/explorer/app.tskb.tsx.explains: ExplorerApp class: boot sequence, render loop, and interaction handlers
docs/src/tskb/explorer/app.tskb.tsx.content:
ExplorerApp <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp>in <nodeId: explorer.spa.main>is the single top-level controller for the explorer SPA.
      All canvas layers, UI state, and callbacks are owned by the class instance. The only public
      surface is <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.mount>— everything else is private, wired internally via callbacks. Boot sequence <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.mount>runs four synchronous setup methods then awaits data. <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupCanvas>  creates the SVG layer stack and wires zoom/pan. <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupTooltips>mounts hover and code
      tooltips. <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupRenderer>constructs the node renderer with bound interaction callbacks.   <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupSearch>wires the search input. <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.loadInitialData>fetches   meta.json and calls store.loadMeta() , which notifies the render
      subscriber and triggers the first <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.render>. Render loop <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.render>is a pure coordinator: it recomputes the layout when dirty, delegates all
      application logic to <nodeId: explorer.spa.computeRenderState>in <nodeId: explorer.spa.render-state>, then passes the result
      straight to the D3 rendering functions. No computation happens inside <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.render>itself —
      the boundary between logic and rendering is <nodeId: explorer.spa.render-state>. Interaction handlers <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.onExpand>handles folder and module expand/collapse. For folder expand it calls   <nodeId: explorer.spa.showNodeSpinner>, fetches via the loader, calls <nodeId: explorer.spa.removeNodeSpinner>in   finally , then updates the expanded set and calls <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.render>. Collapse
      delegates to <nodeId: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.collapseDescendants>to recursively clear all descendants from the

Visualization Located in <nodeId: core.visualization>. Contains <nodeId: visualization.dot-generator>which provides <nodeId: generateDot>. Transforms
      a KnowledgeGraph into Graphviz DOT format — folders become nested subgraphs, modules/exports
      are grouped within their folders, terms and docs get separate clusters. Output is written to

docs/src/tskb/core/graph-builder.tskb.ts.explains: Graph module: data model (types) and construction (builder) of the knowledge graph
docs/src/tskb/core/graph-builder.tskb.ts.content:
Graph Module Located in <nodeId: core.extraction.graph>. Takes extracted registry and documentation, produces a   <nodeId: KnowledgeGraph>. Modules <nodeId: graph.types>: Defines the graph data model. Node types: FolderNode, ModuleNode, TermNode,
        ExportNode, <nodeId: FlowNode>, DocNode. Edge types: references, belongs-to, contains,
        related-to, flow-step. Top-level structure: <nodeId: KnowledgeGraph>with nodes, edges, and
        metadata. <nodeId: graph.builder>: <nodeId: buildGraph>creates nodes from registry items and docs, then builds
        edges — explicit references from docs and inferred hierarchy from folder paths
        (contains/belongs-to). <relation from="buildGraph" to="KnowledgeGraph" /><relation from="buildGraph" to="visualization.dot-generator" />

The package in <nodeId: TSKB.Package.Root>is published to npm. <nodeId: npm.README.md>is the primary documentation

When structural changes are made to the consumer-facing surface — <nodeId: tskb.cli>commands, <nodeId: runtime.jsx>components, <nodeId: runtime.registry>, runtime primitives - $ <nodeId: tskb.runtime>, type interfaces, build output format, or generated artifacts ( <nodeId: cli.utils.skill-generator>, <nodeId: cli.utils.copilot-instructions>) — <nodeId: npm.README.md>in <nodeId: TSKB.Package.Root>must be updated to
      reflect them.

docs/src/tskb/cli/logging.tskb.tsx.explains: CLI logging: stderr-only output, --verbose flag, timing support
docs/src/tskb/cli/logging.tskb.tsx.content:
CLI Logger <nodeId: cli.utils.logger>is a module-level singleton that routes all CLI output to stderr, keeping
      stdout clean for query command results. Design All log output goes to stderr via process.stderr.write — never stdout Configured once at startup in <nodeId: cli.index>via configure( { verbose } ), driven by the
        --verbose CLI flag Log functions: info() always prints, verbose() only when --verbose is set, error() for
        errors, time() prints a label then returns a closure that logs elapsed ms at verbose level Output Modes Query commands (search, pick, context, ls, docs) support three output modes on stdout: Default: pretty-printed JSON (human-readable) --optimized: compact JSON (no whitespace, smaller payload) --plain: structured plain text optimized for AI assistants — no JSON overhead, ~30% fewer
        tokens while preserving all semantic content. Uses plainOut() in the logger Verbosity Default: <nodeId: cli.commands.build>shows key stages and summary counts. Query commands produce output
        only on stdout, nothing on stderr --verbose: build adds timing per pipeline stage, path resolution stats, and file paths.
        Query commands log "Loaded graph from ..." on stderr

docs/src/tskb/cli/cli-overview.tskb.tsx.explains: CLI structure: commands (init, build, search, pick, context, ls, docs, flows) and utils (output generators, content builder, logger)
docs/src/tskb/cli/cli-overview.tskb.tsx.content:
CLI Located in <nodeId: tskb.cli>. Entry point: <nodeId: cli.index>— parses arguments, routes to command
      handlers. Commands In <nodeId: tskb.cli.commands>: <nodeId: cli.commands.init>: Interactive scaffolder — creates docs folder, tsconfig, starter doc, build
        script <nodeId: cli.commands.build>: Full pipeline — files → TypeScript program → extraction → graph → outputs <nodeId: cli.commands.search>: Fuzzy search across all node types, returns ranked JSON results <nodeId: cli.commands.pick>: Resolve any node by ID or path, returns type-specific context <nodeId: cli.commands.ls>: List folder hierarchy with depth control, includes essential docs <nodeId: cli.commands.flows>: List or search flows sorted by priority (constraint → essential →
        supplementary) Utils In <nodeId: tskb.cli.utils>: <nodeId: cli.utils.content-builder>: Produces two markdown bodies — query body and update body (syntax +
        session triggers) — consumed by skill and instructions generators <nodeId: cli.utils.skill-generator>: Generates two Claude Code skills — tskb and tskb-update <nodeId: cli.utils.copilot-instructions>: Generates two Copilot instructions — tskb and tskb-update <nodeId: cli.utils.graph-finder>: Finds .tskb/graph.json from cwd, used by search/pick/ls <nodeId: cli.utils.logger>: Stderr-only logger with info/verbose/error/time — configured once at startup
        via --verbose flag <nodeId: cli.utils.resolve-node>: Resolves any identifier to a graph node via <nodeId: cli.utils.resolve-node.resolveNode>(exact ID →
        path → nearest parent folder). Also provides edge helpers: getNodeEdges,
        findReferencingDocs, findParent, findAllNodesById.

docs/src/taskflow-app/main.tskb.tsx.explains: TaskFlow application overview: architecture layers, patterns, and tech stack
docs/src/taskflow-app/main.tskb.tsx.content:
TaskFlow Application Full-stack task management application demonstrating modern web architecture patterns.
      Three-layer separation: <nodeId: Client>(React), <nodeId: Server>(Node.js), <nodeId: Shared>(TypeScript types). Architecture Layers <nodeId: Client>: React frontend with <nodeId: contextProvider>for state, hooks for data
        fetching, WebSocket for real-time updates. Details in client-overview. <nodeId: Server>: Node.js backend with <nodeId: serviceLayer>and <nodeId: repositoryPattern>.
        Controllers, services, repositories, middleware. Details in server-overview. <nodeId: Shared>: Type definitions shared across client/server ensuring end-to-end type
        safety. API contracts, domain types. Core Patterns <nodeId: repositoryPattern>: Abstract data access through repository interfaces. Details in
        data-layer. <nodeId: serviceLayer>: Business logic isolated from HTTP and data layers. <nodeId: contextProvider>: React Context for state management instead of Redux. See
        adr-context-over-redux. <nodeId: jwt>: Token-based authentication with refresh flow. Details in authentication-system. Technology Stack Frontend: React, TypeScript, Vite Backend: Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL Real-time: WebSocket for notifications and live updates Type Safety: Shared TypeScript types across full stack Domain Models Tasks: Core entity with status, priority, assignments. See task-domain. Projects: Workspace organization with team management. See project-domain. Authentication: User auth with JWT tokens. See auth-domain. Notifications: Real-time updates via WebSocket. See notification-domain. Documentation Navigation High-Level: architecture-overview for complete architectural picture Client: client-overview for frontend architecture and patterns Server: server-overview for backend layering and request flow Data: data-layer for repository pattern implementation Auth: authentication-system for auth flow and JWT handling Decisions: adr/ folder for architectural decision records Rules: constraints/ folder for enforced architectural constraints

docs/src/taskflow-app/constraints/constr.explains: Constraint: services must access data only through repositories, never directly
docs/src/taskflow-app/constraints/constr.content:
Constraint: Service Layer Isolation Services in <nodeId: Services>MUST NOT directly access the database. All data persistence
      operations MUST go through repositories in <nodeId: Repositories>. Rationale Maintains separation of concerns between business logic and data access Enables swapping database implementations without changing services Facilitates testing by allowing repository mocking Prevents SQL injection and enforces parameterized queries Centralizes data access patterns and optimizations Correct Pattern Services receive repository instances via <nodeId: dependencyInjection>in the constructor. ✅ Good Example: <nodeId: TaskService>accepts TaskRepository in constructor Service calls repository.findById() instead of writing SQL Repository handles all database interaction details ❌ Anti-pattern: Service imports Database class directly Service writes raw SQL queries Service accesses database connection pool Enforcement Code reviews should flag direct database access in services Import linting can prevent importing Database in service files All database operations should be traceable to a repository method
